jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car during opening credits sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated into physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Homer crashes and screams
jokes.punchline: Nelson's signature mocking laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Nelson laughing at someone's misfortune is exactly on-brand

jokes.text: Banks are open. Old people are walking the streets with impunity.
jokes.setup: The world is completely different on a weekday morning
jokes.punchline: Old people walking with 'impunity' - misusing fancy word
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds sophisticated but reveals Bart doesn't quite understand what 'impunity' means

jokes.text: And look! Barney's not even drunk yet. Morning, boys. Can't stop to chat. Time is money.
jokes.setup: Bart notes Barney isn't drunk yet
jokes.punchline: Barney acts uncharacteristically business-like and sober
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - Barney being responsible and time-conscious is the opposite of his usual character

jokes.text: those teachers are cooped up in school like morons!
jokes.setup: While we're having fun walking around
jokes.punchline: Teachers are 'morons' for being at teacher conference
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart attitude toward authority figures, though not particularly clever

jokes.text: Ah. No day would be completely wasted without a visit to the old joke shop.
jokes.setup: Bart's enthusiasm for the joke shop
jokes.punchline: Calling the day 'wasted' while doing something fun
jokes.explanation: Mild irony in Bart's phrasing, though not exceptional

jokes.text: [Chuckles] Rookie mistake, Milhouse. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Milhouse presumably falls for a joke shop prank
jokes.punchline: Bart's condescending but friendly 'rookie mistake'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart/Milhouse dynamic - Bart acting superior while Milhouse suffers

jokes.text: Hey, these don't work. Uh- Oh, a lead shirt. I'll take three pairs. Here's my prescription.
jokes.setup: X-ray specs don't work
jokes.punchline: Milhouse has a lead shirt and prescription for X-ray specs
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation - Milhouse being prepared with lead shirt and having a prescription is wonderfully unexpected

jokes.text: Maybe you can help me. I don't think my face is as hilarious as it could be. What can you show me in a nose or forehead accessory?
jokes.setup: Bart wants to buy joke accessories
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment that his face isn't 'as hilarious as it could be'
jokes.explanation: Bart's matter-of-fact approach to making his face more ridiculous is perfectly in character and unexpectedly clinical

jokes.text: If you put dog doo on the suction cups, they'll stick better.
jokes.setup: Presumably discussing fake nose application
jokes.punchline: Practical but disgusting advice about dog feces as adhesive
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's somewhat cheap but delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Milhouse, I'm not gonna take dog doo that's been on the dirty ground and put it on my face. I have a better idea.
jokes.setup: Bart rejects the disgusting suggestion
jokes.punchline: Implies his 'better idea' will be even worse
jokes.explanation: Good setup for what's coming - Bart's 'better idea' will inevitably be worse

jokes.text: I'm awake! I'm awake! I'm a productive member of the team. You can't fire me! I quit! Please! I have a family.
jokes.setup: Bart wakes Homer from a nap
jokes.punchline: Homer's panicked work-related outburst
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer panic response - escalating from denial to quitting to pleading in rapid succession

jokes.text: I was wondering if you had any industrial-strength adhesive I could use on my face.
jokes.setup: Bart needs help with his prank
jokes.punchline: Casual request for dangerous adhesive for his face
jokes.explanation: Bart's matter-of-fact tone about something so dangerous is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You can get a picture of your mommy with a mummy. [Giggling]
jokes.setup: Taking pictures at the Egyptian exhibit
jokes.punchline: Mommy/mummy pun with self-satisfied giggle
jokes.explanation: Classic mom pun - the giggle shows Marge is proud of her wordplay, which makes it endearing despite being groan-worthy

jokes.text: Whoops! There goes Carlsbad Caverns. Oh, well.
jokes.setup: Marge loading film in camera
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of accidentally exposing vacation photos
jokes.explanation: Marge's casual 'oh well' about ruining vacation photos is perfectly understated

jokes.text: In just 20 minutes we'll be in a three-hour line to see the Orb of Isis.
jokes.setup: Marge's excitement about the museum
jokes.punchline: The irony of spending more time in line than traveling there
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the reality of popular museum exhibits

jokes.text: In case of accidental ingestion, consult a mortician.
jokes.setup: Reading warning label on industrial glue
jokes.punchline: Warning to call mortician, not doctor
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation - skipping doctor and going straight to mortician is grimly funny

jokes.text: At least, it would be for most families.
jokes.setup: Calling this an unusual situation
jokes.punchline: Marge acknowledging the Simpsons are uniquely disaster-prone
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Marge breaking the fourth wall to acknowledge their family's pattern of bizarre mishaps

jokes.text: In a few years when you're old enough to drive, then you can take a bus.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking to take the bus alone
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic about needing to drive before taking buses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - the idea that you need to be old enough to drive before taking public transportation is perfectly backwards parental reasoning

jokes.text: Well, you should've thought of that before I glued all this stuff to my face.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about missing the exhibit
jokes.punchline: Bart blaming Lisa for his own glue mishap
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - taking no responsibility and somehow making it Lisa's fault

jokes.text: She said something, but she was kind of in a rush to get Bart to the emergency room.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what Marge said about the bus
jokes.punchline: Lisa's carefully truthful but misleading response
jokes.explanation: Smart character work - Lisa being technically honest while manipulating the situation

jokes.text: Well, if it would put your mind at ease, I could take a limousine. Although, that would cost $200.
jokes.setup: Discussing transportation options
jokes.punchline: Lisa offering expensive alternative to manipulate Homer's choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant manipulation - Lisa using Homer's cheapness against him by making the bus seem responsible

jokes.text: I know you had your heart set on a limo, but sometimes daddies have to say no, honey. I'm afraid you're gonna have to take the bus.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's being the responsible parent
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards - making Lisa take the more dangerous option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - being 'responsible' while doing exactly the wrong thing

jokes.text: Ah, the old number 22. Clean, reliable public transportation. The chariot of the people. The ride of choice for the poor and very poor alike.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to be positive about the bus
jokes.punchline: Escalating from 'poor' to 'very poor'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and Lisa's attempt to romanticize public transportation is endearing

jokes.text: Are you gettir on this next bus, kid? - Yes. - [Whistles] Taxi!
jokes.setup: Bus driver asking if Lisa wants the bus
jokes.punchline: Driver immediately calls a taxi instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag - bus driver abandoning his own bus for a taxi

jokes.text: It's the bus! The bus! Come on! You're gonna miss the bus!
jokes.setup: People running for the bus
jokes.punchline: Frantic chase for public transportation
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy of people desperately chasing buses

jokes.text: Of course not, honey. That seat's for my coin purse.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for a seat
jokes.punchline: Seat reserved for a coin purse instead of a child
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities - treating an inanimate object better than a child

jokes.text: Yes, if you can answer me these questions three. Question the first-
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for another seat
jokes.punchline: Monty Python bridge keeper reference
jokes.explanation: Classic pop culture reference that works in context of bizarre bus passengers

jokes.text: Yeah, Monday, Wednesday, Friday. Tuesday, Thursday, it's the 22-A.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about bus route 22
jokes.punchline: Confusing scheduling system that changes the route
jokes.explanation: Good satire of confusing public transportation systems

jokes.text: Don't make me tap the sign.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for help while lost
jokes.punchline: Driver threatening to tap sign instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect bureaucratic indifference - threatening official procedure instead of basic human help

jokes.text: Aha! It's springtime, so the geese must be flying back north!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to figure out which way is north
jokes.punchline: Using incorrect bird migration knowledge
jokes.explanation: Lisa's book smarts failing her in practical situations is good character comedy

jokes.text: See, Bart? There's another boy who played with glue. Actually, it was a plumbing explosion.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out consequences of glue
jokes.punchline: Doctor's casual correction about plumbing explosion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - doctor's matter-of-fact correction about something much worse

jokes.text: No, ma'am. This is Area 51-A.
jokes.setup: Lisa thinking she found Area 51
jokes.punchline: Same A/non-A confusion as the bus routes
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that extends the A/non-A confusion to military installations, escalating the absurdity

jokes.text: I'm sorry. The location of this location is classified.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking where she is
jokes.punchline: Can't reveal the location of the location
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and perfect bureaucratic absurdity - the recursiveness is funny

jokes.text: I seen it first! Ooh! Girlie Sue's gonna have a elegant weddir feast.
jokes.setup: Cletus finding roadkill
jokes.punchline: Planning an elegant wedding feast with roadkill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character work - combining hillbilly stereotype with genuine sweetness about wedding

jokes.text: Never you mind, Brandine. You just go back to birthir that baby.
jokes.setup: Brandine asking what's happening
jokes.punchline: Casual instruction to continue giving birth
jokes.explanation: Absurd casualness about childbirth while helping a stranger

jokes.text: Them things can go off even after they's dead.
jokes.setup: Warning Lisa about a dead skunk
jokes.punchline: Treating skunk spray like an explosive device
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus wisdom - treating natural phenomena like military ordnance

jokes.text: Young man, there's nothing funny about novelties. I mean, they're humorous and all, but, uh, this is certainly no laughing matter.
jokes.setup: Doctor being serious about joke shop items
jokes.punchline: Contradicting himself about humor while being unintentionally funny
jokes.explanation: Doctor's self-contradiction about humor is perfectly written professional dialogue

jokes.text: Why, if I had $75 for every novelty I've removed - Oh, by the way, I'll need a check for $75.
jokes.setup: Doctor talking about how common this is
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for exactly that amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - the seamless transition from hypothetical to actual payment demand

jokes.text: Oh, heavens, no! It had to be terror sweat.
jokes.setup: Marge asking why not just turn up the heat
jokes.punchline: Doctor insisting on psychological torture
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant - doctor's professional insistence on specifically terror-induced perspiration

jokes.text: It's what the ancient Egyptians call a souvenir.
jokes.setup: Carl explaining his Egyptian hat
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic explanation of modern concept
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronism - attributing modern tourism to ancient civilizations

jokes.text: I mean, she's so smart they hooked her up to a big computer to try to teach it some things. But she had so much knowledge, it overloaded. And then it got really hot and caught on fire!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how smart Lisa is
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous escalating story ending in computer fire
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer storytelling - starts with pride, escalates absurdly, ends with destruction

jokes.text: That never, uh, happened, did it, Homer? Uh, yes.
jokes.setup: Carl questioning Homer's obvious lie
jokes.punchline: Homer's unconvincing confirmation
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to admit he made something up is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Khlav Khalash!
jokes.setup: Vendor selling food to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Unpronounceable foreign food name
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons foreign food vendor with nonsense name

jokes.text: I am at work. This is what I do. Keep it moving, Marge! This isn't a parking lot!
jokes.setup: Marge questioning why Homer isn't at work
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming street searching is his job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer improvisation - instantly creating a fake job and treating his wife like a civilian

jokes.text: Well, then get back to wherever it is you work, whoever you are.
jokes.setup: Burns confronting Homer
jokes.punchline: Burns not recognizing his own employee
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns/Homer dynamic - Burns' complete obliviousness to his employees

jokes.text: Yoink! Ha-ha! Oh- Oh, great! Somebody stop that awful, awful man!
jokes.setup: Snake stealing something
jokes.punchline: Vendor's over-the-top reaction to minor theft
jokes.explanation: Snake's casual 'yoink' paired with vendor's dramatic reaction is good character work

jokes.text: These are for you if you let me use your cherry picker. Well, I've already got some balloons, but they're not this nice. Deal!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to trade balloons for equipment access
jokes.punchline: Worker's childlike reasoning about balloon quality
jokes.explanation: Good adult-child logic applied to professional equipment trade

jokes.text: I'm not normally a praying man, but if you're up there, please save me, Superman.
jokes.setup: Homer in mortal peril
jokes.punchline: Praying to Superman instead of God
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer's pop culture-addled brain choosing Superman over traditional prayer in crisis

jokes.text: Nah, I'll just walk it off.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting doctor after drawbridge closed on his head
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of serious head injury
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer toughness - treating major head trauma like a minor sports injury

jokes.text: Stupid risks are what make life worth living.
jokes.setup: Lisa regretting taking risks
jokes.punchline: Homer's life philosophy about stupid risks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer wisdom - accidentally profound while being completely reckless

jokes.text: What do you say, honey? Feelir stupid? I know I am!
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting breaking into the museum
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic embrace of stupidity
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-aware stupidity and enthusiasm for bad decisions is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, like you're Miss Perfect! Mom, Lisa's making me feel bad! That shut her up.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa making up
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate reversion to sibling warfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - brief reconciliation immediately followed by tactical manipulation

jokes.text: Could you open the window? The cops have Daddy's prints on file.
jokes.setup: Breaking into the museum
jokes.punchline: Lisa casually mentioning Homer's criminal record
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly by innocent Lisa about her father's criminal history

jokes.text: Now I know how Sir Dudley Winthrop felt when he first fell through the ceiling and discovered the Temple of Isis.
jokes.setup: Lisa appreciating the Egyptian exhibit
jokes.punchline: Comparing their break-in to accidental archaeological discovery
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual romanticism shines through as she equates their crime with historical discovery

jokes.text: A velvet rope. Lisa, you can't go this far and then not go further.
jokes.setup: Lisa noting they can't touch the artifact
jokes.punchline: Homer's criminal logic about point of no return
jokes.explanation: Homer's flawed logic about escalating crimes is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's a music box.
jokes.setup: Ancient mysterious orb making sounds
jokes.punchline: Simple, mundane explanation for the mystery
jokes.explanation: Beautiful misdirection - the mysterious ancient artifact is just a music box

jokes.text: Oh! So now it's 'we,' eh?
jokes.setup: Lisa saying 'we uncovered the secret'
jokes.punchline: Homer noting her change from individual to collective credit
jokes.explanation: Homer catching Lisa's shift to shared credit when things go well is good observational humor

jokes.text: Dad, that's the Old Spice song. It is? Well, that's a good song too.
jokes.setup: Homer humming what he thinks is the ancient Egyptian song
jokes.punchline: It's actually a commercial jingle, Homer doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback - Homer's inability to distinguish ancient music from advertising jingles, plus his casual acceptance when corrected
